senses.definition: to behave in way that is expected of someone of a particular age

examples.x: He needs to act his age and take on more adult responsibilities.
It's time for her to act her age and stop behaving like a teenager.
When attending formal events, it's important to act your age and show professionalism.
Despite being in their fifties, they still know how to have fun and act their age when appropriate.
She was praised for acting her age and handling the situation with maturity.

senses.definition: to ensure that financial records are accurate and that debits and credits match

examples.x: The accountant worked diligently to balance the books before the end of the fiscal year.
It's crucial for businesses to balance the books to accurately assess their financial health.
The audit revealed discrepancies that needed to be addressed to properly balance the books.
Small business owners often rely on professional accountants to help them balance the books.
Balancing the books ensures that financial statements provide a true and fair view of a company's financial position.

senses.definition: to potentially involve negative consequences or harm when undertaking a specific action or decision

examples.x: Investing in the stock market can carry a risk of financial loss.
Driving without a seatbelt carries a risk of injury in case of an accident.
Engaging in extreme sports like rock climbing carries a risk of accidents.
Starting a new business always carries a risk, but it can also lead to rewards.

word_list.pronunciation: klňąa äd wňą Ćnz d íňą Ćd ím…ônt

senses.definition: to hinder a person's ability to make clear, rational decisions due to emotional factors, biases, or external influences

examples.x: His strong emotions about the situation clouded his judgment, making it difficult for him to see the facts objectively.
Under the influence of alcohol, people often make poor decisions because it clouds their judgment.
It's crucial to remain calm and objective to avoid letting anger cloud your judgment when making critical choices.

senses.definition: to perform or deliver in a way that goes beyond what was anticipated or hoped for

examples.x: The company's sales exceeded expectations for the quarter.
The team's dedication allowed them to exceed expectations.

senses.definition: to acquire information, understanding, or expertise in a particular subject or field

examples.x: Reading books is an excellent way to gain knowledge about history.
Attending lectures and taking notes helps students gain knowledge in their chosen field of study.
She is actively gaining knowledge about marketing strategies.
Online courses and tutorials are accessible resources for those looking to gain knowledge.
They gained knowledge from their travels.

senses.definition: to understand and accept logical argument, often leading to a change in one's opinion or course of action

examples.x: After a thoughtful conversation, he finally saw reason and agreed to the proposed changes.
She refused initially, but when presented with the evidence, she saw reason and changed her stance.
It took some convincing, but he eventually saw reason and agreed to the compromise.
I hope they will see reason and realize the importance of our environmental conservation efforts.
It may take time, but I believe people will eventually see reason and work together for the common good.

senses.definition: to take actions and make choices that influence and determine the course of one's future or life path

examples.x: Through hard work and determination, she believes she can shape her destiny.
He has a strong desire to shape his destiny by pursuing a career in medicine.
Your decisions and actions today will shape your destiny in the long run.
By setting clear objectives and working towards them, you can actively shape your destiny.

senses.definition: to intentionally seek or provoke an argument, dispute, or disagreement with someone

examples.x: He was in a bad mood and decided to pick a quarrel with his colleague over a minor issue.
Instead of picking a quarrel, it's better to communicate calmly and resolve issues amicably.

senses.definition: to avoid unnecessary effort, work, or inconvenience by taking a simple or efficient course of action

examples.x: Instead of trying to fix it yourself, you can save yourself the trouble and call a professional plumber.
She saved herself the trouble of searching for the information by asking her colleague, who had it readily available.
To save himself the trouble of traffic, he decided to work from home today.
Why not use a calculator to save yourself the trouble of doing complex math by hand?
By setting up automated bill payments, you can save yourself the trouble of remembering due dates.

senses.definition: to develop a liking or preference for something, especially food or beverages

examples.x: I didn't like coffee at first, but I gradually acquired a taste for it.
It took me a while to acquire a taste for sushi, but now I love it.
Acquiring a taste for spicy food can be challenging, but it's worth it.
Some people acquire a taste for fine wines as they explore different varieties.
Over time, he acquired a taste for exotic cuisines from various cultures.

senses.definition: to have an increase in one's body mass

examples.x: She started to gain weight after adopting a more sedentary lifestyle.
Athletes often need to gain weight to build muscle mass and improve their performance.
Weightlifters follow rigorous training and dietary routines to gain weight in muscle mass.
Pregnancy often leads to women gaining weight as their bodies prepare for childbirth.
To address his health concerns, he embarked on a dietary plan to gain weight.

senses.definition: to say something inappropriate at the wrong time

examples.x: At the first meeting I was afraid of speaking out of turn.
I'm sorry if I spoke out of turn, but I thought you already knew.
During the meeting, he spoke out of turn and derailed the discussion by bringing up unrelated topics.
She realized she had spoken out of turn and apologized for making comments during the presentation.
It's important to think before you speak to avoid speaking out of turn in a formal setting.

senses.definition: to begin something again in a different way, without being negatively influenced by past experiences or issues

examples.x: After the difficult breakup, she decided to move to a new city to start afresh.
Completing the project didn't go as planned, so we decided to abandon it and start afresh with a new approach.
Recognizing the need for change, the company decided to rebrand and start afresh with a new image.
Graduating from college marked the opportunity to leave behind academic challenges and start afresh in the professional world.
The failed business venture taught them valuable lessons, and they were determined to cut their losses and start afresh with a new business idea.

senses.definition: to stay faithful or constant to a belief, principle, or commitment, even in the face of challenges or hardships

examples.x: Despite the pressures and criticisms, the journalist remained true to her commitment to ethical reporting.
The artist faced numerous setbacks throughout his career, but he never wavered in his dedication to remaining true to his unique style.
The activist remained true to her principles, even when it meant facing opposition and ostracism from her community.
The scientist remained true to her pursuit of knowledge, even when her research yielded unexpected and controversial results.
The couple remained true to their love for each other, weathering the storms of life and emerging stronger as a result.
